title: Missing Gastonia Teen Found Safe
summary: A 16-year-old missing from Gastonia for over 5 months has been found safe in North Florida following a tip.
charlotte_impact: Highlights the importance of community vigilance and law enforcement efforts in locating missing persons.

title: Warm Weekend Ahead, Dry Spell Continues
summary: The Carolinas face a warm weekend with a persistent dry spell, increasing fire risk due to a dominating high-pressure system.
charlotte_impact: Elevated fire risk poses a threat to property and public safety, requiring heightened awareness and preventative measures.

title: Church Treasurer Accused of $80,000 Embezzlement
summary: A church treasurer in Lincoln County is accused of stealing $80,000 from a Baptist church between June 2025 and February 2026.
charlotte_impact: Breach of trust within a religious organization can erode community faith and financial stability.

title: Concord Seeks Grant for Public Housing Security
summary: Concord is applying for a $250K HUD grant to install security cameras in public housing communities to address safety concerns.
charlotte_impact: Addresses safety concerns in public housing, potentially improving residents' quality of life and reducing crime.

title: Abandoned Puppies Reunited with Mother
summary: Eight puppies abandoned in Gaston County were reunited with their mother; charges are pending against a suspect.
charlotte_impact: Animal cruelty is a serious issue, and this reunion highlights the importance of animal welfare and responsible pet ownership.

title: Shooting in Northeast Charlotte Injures One
summary: One person was injured in a shooting on McCullough Drive near North Tryon Street in Northeast Charlotte.
charlotte_impact: Gun violence remains a concern in Charlotte, impacting community safety and requiring law enforcement response.

title: Race to Honor Fallen Officer with Scholarship
summary: Newton is hosting a race to raise money for a scholarship in honor of Officer Alden Elliott, killed in a 2024 ambush.
charlotte_impact: Honors the sacrifice of a fallen officer and supports students, fostering community spirit and remembrance.

title: Final Defendant Guilty in 2020 Robbery Case
summary: The final defendant pleads guilty in a deadly 2020 Charlotte robbery case, bringing closure to the victim's family after six years.
charlotte_impact: Provides closure for the victim's family and reinforces the justice system's commitment to holding criminals accountable.

title: Fatal House Fire in Caldwell County
summary: A house fire in Caldwell County on Spring Valley Place resulted in one fatality.
charlotte_impact: House fires pose a significant risk to life and property, highlighting the importance of fire safety measures.

title: Airport Building Trails for Animal Shelter
summary: Charlotte airport is building new trails for Charlotte-Mecklenburg Animal Care & Control due to construction concerns.
charlotte_impact: Ensures continued support for animal welfare despite airport construction, benefiting shelter dogs.

title: Two Arrested After Horse Struck by Car
summary: Two men were arrested after a horse was struck by a car in Uptown Charlotte on April 4 near 7th and College Street.
charlotte_impact: Highlights the dangers of reckless driving and the importance of animal safety in urban environments.

title: CMPD Officer Charged with DWI
summary: A CMPD officer, Dylan Auch, was arrested in Locust for DWI and an expired vehicle registration on Wednesday.
charlotte_impact: Law enforcement officers are held to a high standard, and DWI charges can erode public trust.

title: Man Charged After Arson Threat
summary: A man was charged after allegedly threatening to burn down a Burke County business after pouring gasoline on the floor.
charlotte_impact: Arson threats pose a serious danger to life and property, requiring immediate law enforcement intervention.

title: Suspect Surrenders in Parking Lot Shooting
summary: A suspect wanted for shooting into a crowded gas station parking lot in Kings Mountain has surrendered to police.
charlotte_impact: Reduces immediate threat to public safety and allows the legal process to proceed.

title: Blue Line Single-Tracking for Construction
summary: CATS will single-track the Blue Line from April 19 to May 8 for South End Station construction, causing service disruptions.
charlotte_impact: Informs commuters about upcoming transit disruptions, allowing them to plan accordingly.
